title: MPG to L/100km Converter
description: Industrial-grade converter from miles per gallon (MPG) to litres per 100 kilometres (L/100km) with embedded quality, efficiency, and environmental loss analysis. Compliant with ISO 14040, Six Sigma DMAIC, and WERC DC metrics.

inputs.label: Fuel Economy (MPG)
inputs.businessContext: Primary input: miles per gallon (US gallon). Typical range for passenger vehicles: 10–60 mpg. Used in North American fleet reporting.

businessContext_i18n.en: Primary input: miles per gallon (US gallon). Typical range for passenger vehicles: 10–60 mpg. Used in North American fleet reporting.

inputs.label: Fuel Type
inputs.businessContext: Determines carbon intensity and energy content. Required for environmental loss calculations per ISO 14067.

businessContext_i18n.en: Determines carbon intensity and energy content. Required for environmental loss calculations per ISO 14067.

inputs.label: Driving Cycle
inputs.businessContext: Affects real-world efficiency deviation. Used in Six Sigma process capability analysis for fleet variance.

businessContext_i18n.en: Affects real-world efficiency deviation. Used in Six Sigma process capability analysis for fleet variance.

inputs.label: Annual Mileage
inputs.businessContext: Used to compute total fuel consumption, cost, and emissions. Typical fleet vehicle: 10k–30k miles/year.

businessContext_i18n.en: Used to compute total fuel consumption, cost, and emissions. Typical fleet vehicle: 10k–30k miles/year.

inputs.label: Fuel Price per Gallon
inputs.businessContext: Local fuel cost for total cost of ownership (TCO) analysis. Input for Lean cost reduction projects.

businessContext_i18n.en: Local fuel cost for total cost of ownership (TCO) analysis. Input for Lean cost reduction projects.

inputs.label: CO₂ per Gallon
inputs.businessContext: Default for gasoline: 8.887 kg CO₂/gallon (EPA). Used for environmental impact assessment per ISO 14064.

businessContext_i18n.en: Default for gasoline: 8.887 kg CO₂/gallon (EPA). Used for environmental impact assessment per ISO 14064.

rules.message: MPG value outside typical fleet range. Verify input or check for measurement error.
Diesel CO₂ per gallon typically > 9.0 kg. Please verify emission factor.
City MPG > 40 is rare for conventional vehicles. Consider hybrid/electric classification.

thresholds.message: Fuel consumption exceeds 12 L/100km — investigate vehicle maintenance or driving behaviour.
Annual fuel cost exceeds $3,000 — consider alternative fuel or route optimisation.
Annual CO₂ exceeds 5 tonnes — review fleet carbon reduction targets (ISO 14064).

formulas.name: Litres per 100 km
formulas.description: Primary conversion from MPG (US) to L/100km.

formulas.name: Annual Fuel Consumption (gallons)
formulas.description: Total gallons consumed per year based on annual mileage.

formulas.name: Annual Fuel Cost
formulas.description: Total cost of fuel per year.

formulas.name: Annual CO₂ Emissions (kg)
formulas.description: Total CO₂ emitted per year based on fuel type and consumption.

formulas.name: Annual CO₂ Emissions (tonnes)
formulas.description: Converted to metric tonnes for reporting.

formulas.name: Efficiency Loss Factor
formulas.description: Six Sigma-based deviation from ideal fuel economy (ideal = 40 mpg).

formulas.name: Environmental Loss Index
formulas.description: Composite index combining fuel consumption and CO₂ intensity, normalised to 0–100.

primary.label: Fuel Consumption (L/100km)
primary.description: Primary converted value from MPG to L/100km.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Driving Cycle Penalty
hiddenLossDrivers.description: City driving increases fuel consumption by 15–30% vs combined cycle.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Fuel Type Energy Density
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Ethanol and biodiesel have lower energy density, increasing L/100km by 5–10%.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Vehicle Age Efficiency Degradation
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Older vehicles lose 1–2% fuel efficiency per year due to engine wear.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Tire Pressure Deviation
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Under-inflated tires increase rolling resistance and fuel consumption by up to 3%.

suggestedActions.label: Optimise Driving Behaviour
suggestedActions.description: Implement eco-driving training to reduce fuel consumption by 5–15%.

suggestedActions.label: Switch to Alternative Fuel
suggestedActions.description: Consider hybrid or electric vehicles for high-mileage routes.
suggestedActions.expectedImprovement: 30-60% reduction in fuel cost and CO₂

suggestedActions.label: Regular Maintenance
suggestedActions.description: Schedule engine tune-ups, air filter replacement, and tire pressure checks.

suggestedActions.label: Route Optimisation
suggestedActions.description: Use telematics to avoid congested routes and reduce idling.

dataConfidenceAdjusted.label: Data Confidence Adjusted Value
dataConfidenceAdjusted.description: Weighted confidence score (0–100) based on input completeness, consistency, and validation rule compliance. Higher score indicates higher reliability of outputs.
dataConfidenceAdjusted.calculation: base_score = 100; subtract 10 for each validation warning, 20 for each error, 5 for each info alert. Clamped between 0 and 100.
